title: Question 1.1
statement_text: Given a graph class $\mathcal{G}$, is there a constant $c_{\mathcal{G}}$ such that $$\frac{\gamma(G)}{\rho(G)}\leqslant c_{\mathcal{G}}\quad\text{ for each }\quad G\in\mathcal{G}~{}~{}?$$
context_text: The paper studies the ratio between the dominating number $\gamma(G)$ and the packing number $\rho(G)$ of a graph $G$. A positive answer immediately gives the bound $c_{\mathcal{G}}$ on the integrality gap of both the dominating and the packing integer programs for any $G\in\mathcal{G}$.
paper_title: On graph classes with constant domination-packing ratio

title: Conjecture 1.2 (Henning, Löwenstein, and Rautenbach)
statement_text: Let $G$ be a connected graph with $\Delta(G)\leqslant 3$, then $\gamma(G)\leqslant 2\rho(G)+1$. The equality holds only for three well-defined graphs, one of which being the Petersen graph.
context_text: Henning, Löwenstein, and Rautenbach showed that if $\Delta(G)\leqslant 2$ then $\gamma(G)\leqslant\rho(G)+1$, and that if $G$ is claw-free with $\Delta(G)\leqslant 3$ then $\gamma(G)\leqslant 2\rho(G)$. They then stated this conjecture for general subcubic graphs. The general upper bound $\gamma(G)\leqslant\Delta(G)\rho(G)$ serves as the trivial baseline.
paper_contribution: The paper sharpens Conjecture 1.2, improving related constants and providing new structural context through results on 2-degenerate graphs and other graph classes.
paper_title: On graph classes with constant domination-packing ratio

title: Question 9.1
statement_text: Characterize those graph classes $\mathcal{G}$ for which there is a constant $c_{\mathcal{G}}$ such that $\dfrac{\gamma(G)}{\rho(G)}\leqslant c_{\mathcal{G}}$ for each $G\in\mathcal{G}$.
context_text: In the conclusion, after extending both the list of graph classes with bounded $\gamma/\rho$ ratio and those with unbounded ratio (notably showing 2-degenerate graphs have bounded ratio), the authors note that Theorem 6.1 on 2-degenerate graphs rules out any characterization purely in terms of sparse graph classes.
notes: Statement text is partially cut off in the provided content; the formula is inferred from context and from the identical formulation in Question 1.1.
paper_title: On graph classes with constant domination-packing ratio
